# ShellCommandJobComponent

Run a shell command as a Dagster job (no asset materialized) — for cleanup, maintenance, ad-hoc tasks.

## Dependencies
(none)

<!-- FIELDS:START - auto-generated by tools/regen_readme_fields.py -->

## Fields

### Required

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `job_name` | `str` | Dagster job name |
| `command` | `str` | Shell command to execute (passed to `bash -c`) |

### Execution

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `timeout_seconds` | `int` | `600` | Timeout in seconds |

### Catalog metadata

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tags` | `dict` | — | Dagster job tags |

### Sensor configuration

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `default_status` | `str` | `"STOPPED"` | STOPPED \| RUNNING |

### Other

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `schedule` | `str` | — | Cron schedule (None = no schedule) |
| `working_dir` | `str` | — | cwd for the command |
| `env_vars` | `dict` | — | Extra environment variables |
| `fail_on_nonzero` | `bool` | `true` | Fail the job if exit code != 0 |

<!-- FIELDS:END -->

## What this is
A **Dagster job** (no asset materialized) — the component installs a `dg.job`
plus an optional schedule. Lineage graph stays clean; the work happens
behind the scenes.
